SFP Help
Overview
Settings
General Notifications Catalog Material Types Audiences Statuses Users Groups Backups
SFP Settings Guide

Settings Guide

How to configure SFP — messages patrons see, catalog connections, statuses, users, and more.

Overview

Settings are only accessible to Admin users. You get there from the navigation bar — look for your name or avatar in the top right, or go directly via Settings in the nav. The sidebar inside Settings lets you jump between nine areas: General, Notifications, Catalog, Material Types, Audiences, Statuses, Users, Groups, and Backups.

Most changes take effect immediately after saving. A few (like messaging changes) are visible to patrons as soon as you save.

General

Controls patron PIN login, request limits, ILL messaging, and the messages patrons see at key moments during submission.

Patron

SettingWhat it does
Enable Patron PIN LoginWhen on, a "Sign in with your library card PIN" button appears on the submission form. Patrons can authenticate with their barcode and PIN to view their submitted requests and statuses at /my-requests.

Request Limits

Controls how many requests a patron can submit and how the limit window is measured.

SettingWhat it does
Request Limit CountHow many requests a patron can submit within the active window. Default: 5.
Limit Window Type How the window is measured. Choose one of three modes:
  • Rolling — counts requests within a sliding day window (e.g. any 30-day period). Configure the window length below.
  • Calendar Month — resets on the same day every month (e.g. the 1st). Configure the reset day below.
  • Calendar Week — resets every Monday.
Rolling Window LengthOnly shown when Rolling is selected. The number of days in the sliding window. Default: 30.
Monthly Reset DayOnly shown when Calendar Month is selected. The day of the month the counter resets (1–28). Default: 1.
💡
When a patron hits the limit, both the submission form and the My Requests view show a message indicating when they can submit again.

ILL (Interlibrary Loan)

SettingWhat it does
ILL Age ThresholdIf the item's publish date is older than this many days, the patron sees the ILL Warning Message during submission. Default: 730 days (2 years).
ILL Warning MessageThe message shown to patrons when their item is older than the threshold. Supports rich text formatting.

Messaging

These are the messages patrons see at various points. All support rich text (bold, links, etc.).

SettingWhen it appears
Submission Success MessageShown after a patron's request is successfully submitted.
Catalog Owned MessageShown when the patron confirms the catalog already has the item — no request is created.
Duplicate Request MessageShown when a different patron has already requested the same title.
Duplicate Self-Request MessageShown when a patron submits something they've already requested themselves.
Auto-Order Author Exclusion MessageShown when a patron submits a future release by an author on the auto-order exclusion list — no request is created.

Auto-Order Author Exclusions

A plain-text list of author names, one per line. When a patron submits a title with a future release date and the author matches this list, SFP shows the exclusion message instead of creating a request. This is useful for high-demand authors whose new titles you always pre-order automatically.

💡
The exclusion only triggers when the item has a specific future release date (like 2025-09-15) — not just a year. Vague dates won't trigger it.

Syndetics

SettingWhat it does
Syndetics Client IDYour library's Syndetics client ID, used to display book cover images during patron submission.

Notifications

Controls all automated emails SFP sends — to selectors when new requests arrive, and to patrons when their request status changes.

Master switch

SettingWhat it does
Enable NotificationsGlobal on/off for all SFP emails. Turn this off to silence everything without touching individual settings.

Staff routing emails

Sent to selector groups when a new request is submitted. Routing uses the request's material type and audience to find the matching group(s) and sends to each group's configured email address.

SettingWhat it does
Enable Staff Routing EmailsTurn off to stop routing emails without disabling patron notifications.
Staff Routing — SubjectSubject line for routing emails. Supports placeholders like {title}, {author}, {material_type}.
Staff Routing — Email BodyHTML body for routing emails. Leave blank to use the built-in default template.

Patron status emails

Sent to the patron when their request moves to a status that has Notify Patron checked. The patron must have an email address on file.

SettingWhat it does
Enable Patron Status EmailsTurn off to stop patron emails without disabling staff routing.
Patron Status — SubjectSubject line for patron status emails. Supports the same placeholders as staff routing.
Patron Status — Email BodyHTML body for patron emails. Leave blank to use the built-in default template.
💡
Available placeholders for subjects and bodies: {title}, {author}, {patron_name}, {patron_first_name}, {material_type}, {audience}, {status}, {submitted_date}, {request_url}.
💡
To control which statuses trigger a patron email, edit the status under Settings → Statuses and check the Notify Patron option on that status.

Catalog

Controls how SFP connects to your catalog and external metadata sources during patron submission.

Catalog Search

SettingWhat it does
Enable Catalog SearchWhen on, SFP searches the catalog during submission so patrons can be redirected to place a hold if the item is already owned.
Bibliocommons Library SlugYour library's subdomain on Bibliocommons (e.g. dcpl for dcpl.bibliocommons.com).
Catalog Search URL TemplateThe URL pattern used to link patrons to catalog search results. Uses {slug} and {query} as placeholders.

ISBNdb

SettingWhat it does
Enable ISBNdb SearchWhen on, SFP looks up the title in ISBNdb after submission to enrich the record with ISBN, publisher, and publication date. Only runs when the catalog search returns no results.

Post-Submit Processing Mode

ValueBehavior
waitThe patron's browser waits on the page while catalog and ISBNdb lookups run. Fastest feedback for the patron.
emailThe patron sees a confirmation immediately and lookups run in the background. A follow-up email is sent when processing completes.

BiblioCommons Format Labels

Bibliocommons returns raw format codes (like BK or MUSIC_CD) in catalog results. This table lets you map those codes to friendlier display labels shown to patrons (e.g. BK → Book). Click Remove to delete a mapping, or use the form at the bottom to add a new one.

Material Types

Material types are the item categories patrons choose when submitting a request — for example Book, DVD, or Audiobook. Patrons see these as a dropdown on the submission form.

Click + New Material Type to add one. Click Edit on any row to change the name or toggle it active/inactive. Inactive types won't appear on the patron submission form or in filters.

⚠️
Deleting a material type will remove it from any requests that used it. It's better to set it Inactive to hide it without losing history.

Audiences

Audiences let patrons indicate who the item is for — Adult, Teen, Kids, etc. Like material types, patrons pick from these during submission.

Click + New Audience to add one, or Edit to rename or deactivate an existing audience. Inactive audiences are hidden from the submission form.

Statuses

Statuses are the labels you apply to requests as you process them — for example Pending, On Order, or Purchased. You can customize them to match your workflow.

Status fields

FieldWhat it does
NameThe label shown in the interface, e.g. On Order.
ColorThe badge color used to visually distinguish this status in lists and detail pages. Use the color picker or type a hex code.
Sort OrderControls the order statuses appear in dropdowns. Lower numbers appear first.
TerminalMark this if the status represents a final state (e.g. Purchased, Denied). Terminal statuses can be used to filter out "closed" requests.
ActiveOnly active statuses appear in the Update Status dropdown. Inactive statuses are hidden from selectors but preserved in history.
💡
Default statuses (Pending, Under Review, On Order, Purchased, Denied, ILL Referred) are seeded automatically. You can rename, recolor, or reorder them freely.

Users

Staff members who log into SFP via Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) are added here automatically the first time they sign in. You don't need to create accounts manually — just have staff log in and then assign their role here.

Roles

RoleAccess
Admin Full access to all requests, patrons, titles, and all Settings pages. Can manage users, groups, statuses, and configuration.
Selector Access to Requests, Patrons, and Titles. Scoped to their assigned Groups — they only see requests that match their group's material types and audiences.

Editing a user

Click Edit on any user to change their role, assign them to selector groups, or toggle their account active/inactive. Setting a user to Inactive prevents them from logging in without deleting their record.

💡
A selector with no group assignments can log in but won't see any requests in their queue. Always assign at least one group after changing a user's role to Selector.

Groups

Groups control which requests each selector sees. A group is a combination of material types and audiences — selectors assigned to a group only see requests that match both.

For example, a group called Adult Books might include the Book material type and the Adult audience. Selectors in that group would only see adult book requests — not DVDs, not teen materials, etc.

Creating or editing a group

  1. Click + New Group (or Edit on an existing one).
  2. Give it a name and optional description.
  3. Check the material types and audiences this group should cover.
  4. Leave Active checked so it appears in user assignment dropdowns.
  5. Click Create Group (or Save Changes).
💡
A selector can belong to multiple groups. Their request queue will show requests matching any of their assigned groups.
⚠️
If you deactivate a group, selectors assigned to it lose that scope from their queue. Reassign them to another group if needed.

Backups

The Backups page provides tools for exporting, importing, and restoring SFP data. It is divided into three areas: Configuration, Database & Storage, and Backup Schedule.

Configuration

Export or import a JSON snapshot of all SFP settings, statuses, material types, audiences, selector groups, and catalog format labels. Use this to clone a configuration to another environment or to recover after accidental changes.

ActionWhat it does
Export ConfigDownloads a .json file containing all current settings and lookup table data.
Import ConfigUpload a previously exported JSON file to restore or overwrite configuration. Existing records are upserted — nothing is deleted.

Database & Storage

Full data export and restore tools. Use with caution — these operate on the entire SFP database.

ActionWhat it does
Export DatabaseDownloads a full SQL dump of all SFP tables, including requests, patrons, and titles.
Export StorageDownloads a ZIP of the storage/app directory.
Import DatabaseRestore the database from an uploaded SQL file. Replaces all existing data.
⚠️
The Wipe All Data option permanently truncates all SFP tables. You must type WIPE to confirm. This cannot be undone — export first.

Backup Schedule

Configure an automated backup cron job — choose frequency, what to include (config, database, storage), and where to save files. The schedule runs via Laravel's task scheduler; ensure a cron entry is set up on the server for php artisan schedule:run.
